# Error Handling Checklist: Non-Stopping Reviewability Markers

**Purpose**: Validate that non-stopping reviewability behavior is specified without weakening correctness, safety, verification, or PR-packet stops.
**Created**: 2026-06-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md) | [contracts/](../contracts/)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the post-G5 task reviewability outcomes that may continue explicitly limited to parseable `pass`, `warn`, honored `exception`, and size-only `status=block` evidence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the plan require the post-G5 `reviewability-gate.sh tasks` invocation to guard against `set -e`/`pipefail`, capture stdout and exit code, and branch on parsed JSON instead of letting exit 1 stop implementation? [Resolved, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK003 Does the plan define how to handle valid task-mode `status=block` JSON that exits nonzero without changing the lower-level `reviewability-gate.sh tasks` caller contract? [Resolved, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK004 Are final-backstop requirements defined for both the proceed path (`marker_split` with a valid current marker plan) and the stop path (missing, stale, malformed, or fingerprint-mismatched marker plan)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK005 Are malformed plans, unreadable plan/task artifacts, invalid JSON, missing reviewability status or mode, and unusable gate evidence all listed as correctness stops? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK006 Does the plan define the stop/proceed decision matrix tightly enough that a generic nonzero exit, invalid evidence, or unknown reviewability status cannot be treated as marker input? [Resolved, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "size-only block" defined clearly enough to distinguish reviewability budget findings from correctness, safety, verification, or packet-validation failures? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Data Model §Reviewability Finding]
- [ ] CHK008 Is `marker_split` named as a final pre-PR proceed outcome with successful exit behavior and evidence passed to marker-based PR emission? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, Contract §marker-split-result.schema.json]
- [ ] CHK009 Are invalid PR packets required to stop before `gh pr create` or any equivalent PR side effect, rather than being rendered as warnings in a PR body? [Resolved, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the distinction between reviewability warnings, structured marker warnings, and blocking errors clear enough for PR packet generation? [Clarity, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Data Model §Marker Evidence]
- [ ] CHK011 Are stale marker fingerprints defined as correctness stops at resume and final emission, not as warnings that still proceed? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Are post-G5 task gate handling, final-backstop handling, and multi-PR emission all consistent on the same allowlist of non-stopping reviewability conditions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK013 Does keeping `reviewability-gate.sh tasks` compatible align with the plan's caller-side capture-and-interpret behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK014 Are Codex mirror requirements included for the error-handling wording that tells agents not to stop for size alone while still stopping for correctness failures? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK015 Are hazard-collapse requirements consistent with non-stopping implementation and one-PR emission warnings, rather than becoming a manual stop? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK016 Are requirements defined for a task reviewability command that exits nonzero but emits valid size-only `status=block` JSON? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK017 Are requirements defined for a task reviewability command that emits malformed JSON, omits status or mode, or points at stale/unreadable evidence? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK018 Are requirements defined for a final full-diff size block with a valid marker plan, and for the same block with no valid marker plan? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK019 Are failed verification, invalid PR packets, and unsafe output covered as stops after implementation has otherwise succeeded? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK020 Are resume scenarios covered where prior marker evidence exists but fingerprints or marker membership no longer match current inputs? [Coverage, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK021 Do success criteria require deterministic fixtures proving non-stopping behavior only when reviewability size is the sole negative finding? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK022 Do success criteria require correctness-stop fixtures for malformed plans, failed verification, invalid PR packets, unsafe output, unusable gate evidence, stale fingerprints, and missing or malformed marker state? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK023 Does verification include final-backstop `marker_split` behavior and marker-based PR emission, not only marker planning? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK024 Are expected exit/status outcomes measurable for task-gate block handling and final-backstop marker split behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-012]

## Notes

- Items carrying a Gap marker require spec.md and/or plan.md remediation before G4 can pass.

## Re-Run Verification

- [ ] CHK025 Does the updated plan include a guarded post-G5 task-gate invocation pattern that captures stdout and exit code under `set -euo pipefail`? [Resolved, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK026 Does the updated plan preserve the lower-level `reviewability-gate.sh tasks` exit-code contract while allowing only valid size-only task blocks to proceed as marker input? [Resolved, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK027 Does the updated plan define a closed stop/proceed matrix so code 2, invalid JSON, unknown statuses, stale evidence, unreadable artifacts, and non-size blockers remain correctness stops? [Resolved, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK028 Does the updated spec and plan require marker-aware PR packet validation before PR body generation, `gh pr create`, or equivalent PR side effects? [Resolved, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
